content:
# Colossians Chapter 3 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Date:** 2026-03-05

---

### v.1 — "seek the things above" vs. "set your sights on the realities of heaven"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "seek the things above, where the Christos is, sitting at [the] right [hand] of God" | ta anō zēteite |
| LOGOS | "seek the things that are above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "set your sights on the realities of heaven, where Christ sits in the place of honor at God's right hand" | "Realities of heaven" — adds "realities"; "place of honor" — adds |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Paul says "the things above" (ta anō) — not "the realities of heaven." PNEUMA adds "realities" and replaces "right hand" with "place of honor" — an interpretation, not a translation.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "realities" and "place of honor."

---

### v.5 — "the members which are upon the earth" vs. "what is earthly in you" vs. "the sinful, earthly things lurking within you"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the members which [are] upon the earth" | ta melē ta epi tēs gēs — "the members on the earth" |
| LOGOS | "what is earthly in you" | Renders abstractly |
| PNEUMA | "the sinful, earthly things lurking within you" | Adds "sinful" and "lurking within you" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Paul says "the members on the earth" — the earthly aspects that must be killed. LOGOS abstracts appropriately. PNEUMA adds "sinful" (not in Greek) and "lurking within you" — personalizing and dramatizing.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "sinful" and "lurking."

---

### v.6 — "sons of disobedience" vs. absence

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the wrath of God comes upon the sons of disobedience" | tous huious tēs apeitheias |
| LOGOS | "the wrath of God is coming upon the sons of disobedience" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "the anger of God is coming" | Drops "upon the sons of disobedience" entirely |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. PNEUMA drops "upon the sons of disobedience" — the same Hebraism as Ephesians 5:6. The phrase identifies those upon whom wrath falls. Note: some manuscripts omit this phrase in Colossians 3:6; Sinaiticus includes it.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA drops the Hebraism (textual note: phrase present in Sinaiticus).

---

### v.9-10 — "old man/new man" vs. "old self/new self" vs. "old sinful nature/new nature"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "having put off the old man...having put on the new [man]" | ton palaion anthrōpon...ton neon |
| LOGOS | "you have put off the old self...put on the new self" | "Self" — avoids gendered "man" |
| PNEUMA | "you have stripped off your old sinful nature...Put on your new nature" | "Sinful nature" / "new nature" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Same issue as Ephesians 4:22-24. PNEUMA renders anthrōpos as "nature" — importing a component anthropology where Paul uses clothing/identity language. Paul's metaphor is of stripping off and putting on entire persons, not swapping inner natures.

**Status:** ⚠️ Theologically significant. Same anthropological issue as Ephesians 4:22-24 and Galatians 5. Paul's "old man/new man" is an identity category, not an inner component. PNEUMA's "sinful nature/new nature" continues the systematic reframing.

---

### v.10 — "being renewed in knowledge after the image of its creator" vs. "be renewed as you learn to know your Creator and become like him"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the [one] being renewed unto knowledge according to [the] image of the [one] having created him" | anakainoumenon eis epignōsin kat' eikona tou ktisantos auton |
| LOGOS | "which is being renewed in knowledge after the image of its creator" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "be renewed as you learn to know your Creator and become like him" | "Learn to know" — process; "become like him" — adds |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Paul says the new self is "being renewed into knowledge according to the image of the one who created it." PNEUMA splits this into two actions ("learn to know" + "become like him") and adds "become like him" — the Greek says "according to the image," not "become like."

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA splits and adds.

---

### v.12 — "bowels of compassion" vs. "compassionate hearts" vs. "tenderhearted mercy"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "bowels of compassion" | splanchna oiktirmou — "innards of compassion" |
| LOGOS | "compassionate hearts" | "Hearts" — English idiom for splanchna |
| PNEUMA | "tenderhearted mercy" | "Tenderhearted mercy" — abstract |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. splanchna (literally "intestines/bowels") was the Greek seat of emotion — equivalent to English "heart." LOGOS renders with the English emotional equivalent. PNEUMA's "tenderhearted mercy" is adequate but abstracts away the concrete body image.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. Minor — all capture the sense.

---

### v.16 — "the word of the Christos" vs. "the word of Christ" vs. "the message about Christ"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the word of the Christos" | ho logos tou Christou |
| LOGOS | "the word of Christ" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "the message about Christ, in all its richness" | "Message about" — specifies genitive; "in all its richness" — moves plousiōs |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. "The word of Christ" preserves the genitive ambiguity (Christ's word, or the word about Christ). PNEUMA specifies as "message about Christ" (objective genitive only) and repositions "richly" (plousiōs) as "in all its richness" — expanding an adverb into a prepositional phrase.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA resolves ambiguous genitive.

---

### v.22 — "slaves" vs. "bondservants" — douloi (same as Ephesians 6)

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "Slaves, obey in all things the lords according to [the] flesh" | douloi |
| LOGOS | "Bondservants, obey in everything those who are your earthly masters" | "Bondservants" |
| PNEUMA | "Slaves, obey your earthly masters in everything you do" | "Slaves"; adds "you do" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Same rendering as Ephesians 6:5 — "bondservants" preserves the kyrios wordplay.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. Consistent with Ephesians 6.

---

## Summary of Disagreement Patterns

| Pattern | Count | Description |
|---|---|---|
| PNEUMA interpretive additions | 3 | "Sinful/lurking" (v.5), "realities/place of honor" (v.1), "become like him" (v.10) |
| PNEUMA "sinful nature" | 1 | v.9-10 — same pattern as Galatians, Ephesians |
| PNEUMA drops elements | 1 | "Sons of disobedience" dropped (v.6) |
| PNEUMA resolves ambiguity | 1 | "Word of Christ" → "message about Christ" (v.16) |

**Overall pattern:** Chapter 3's most significant disagreement is again PNEUMA's "sinful nature/new nature" rendering (vv.9-10) — the same systematic reframing seen in Galatians 5, Ephesians 4, and Colossians 2. Paul's clothing metaphor (put off the old person, put on the new person) describes a change of entire identity, not a swap of inner components. This is now a cross-letter pattern that affects Paul's anthropology consistently.

---

*— SOPHIA, AI Translator, in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
